back_translation: When anyone brings a grain offering to the Lord, his offering must be fine flour. He shall pour oil on the flour and place frankincense on the flour.

And he shall bring that offering to the sons of Aaron, the priests, and the priest shall take a full handful from the flour and from the oil, together with all the frankincense of the offering, and shall burn it as a memorial portion on the altar, an offering by fire, a pleasing aroma to the Lord.
And the remainder of the grain offering shall belong to Aaron and to his sons; it is a most-holy portion from the offerings by fire to the Lord.
And when you shall present a grain offering baked in the oven, it must be fine flour made into unleavened cakes mixed with oil, or unleavened wafers coated with oil.
And if your offering is a grain offering prepared on a flat-griddle, it must be unleavened bread made of fine flour mixed with oil.
You shall break the offering into pieces and pour oil on it; that is a grain offering.
And if your offering is a grain offering prepared in a deep pan, it must be made of fine flour with oil.
And you shall bring the grain offering that is made from these to present to the Lord; you shall give it to the priest, and he shall bring it to the altar.
And the priest shall take the memorial portion from the grain offering and burn it as smoke on the altar, an offering by fire, a pleasing aroma to the Lord.
And the remainder of the grain offering shall belong to Aaron and to his sons; it is a most-holy portion from the offerings by fire to the Lord.
Any grain offering that you shall present to the Lord must not be made with leaven, for you shall not burn any leaven or any honey as an offering by fire to the Lord.
You shall present these things to the Lord as a firstfruits offering, but they must not go up on the altar as a pleasing aroma.
And you shall season every grain offering of yours with salt; you must not neglect the salt of the covenant of your God from your grain offering — with all your offerings you shall present salt.
And if you shall present a grain offering of firstfruits to the Lord, you shall present fresh-grain heads roasted on fire, crushed from new grain, as your grain offering of firstfruits.
And you shall put oil on the offering, and place frankincense on the offering; that is a grain offering.
And the priest shall burn the memorial portion from the crushed grain and from its oil, together with all its frankincense, as an offering by fire to the Lord.
